title: Poor feeding

items.final_wording: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies weight and height measurement, clinical/developmental/social assessment, detailed feeding or eating history, observation, and assessment-directed investigations.

items.rationale: Directly supports mapped feeding history, weight or growth, measurement, observation, and assessment-directed investigation fields.

items.final_wording: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies suckling, bottle feeding, routines, environment, aversion, interaction, appetite, food types, and feeding-diary domains.

items.rationale: Directly supports mapped breastfeeding, formula, routines, appetite, aversion, feeding-environment, and structured-history domains.

items.final_wording: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

source.title: Faltering growth: recognition and management of faltering growth in children — Recommendations
source.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines growth monitoring and referral for underlying disorder, failure to respond, slow growth, rapid weight loss, severe undernutrition, safeguarding concern, or acute illness.

items.rationale: Directly supports mapped weight-loss concern, monitoring, follow-up, and clinician-arranged referral documentation.
